segments.text: Hey, incoming. My name is Madison Schmidt. I'm with the Nebraska Clean Cities and Communities Coalition.

The Clean Cities and Communities Partnership is from the Department of Energy and the network of coalitions across the nation that are committed to promoting and advancing clean transportation in a safe manner,
which is why we're here today to learn about electric vehicles and how to approach them safely during emergencies.
Our instructor today is Barry Smith from Southeast Community College. He's the program director of automotive technology here.
We're just going to get right into it. Thanks so much.
Hi, everybody. I'm Barry Smith. I've been teaching for Southeast Community College for the last 23 years.
And for about the last 10, I've taught most of the EV hybrid related classes for them.
I worked for Honda in Lincoln when we got our first Honda Insight back in 2000.
And so I've been kind of dabbling with EVs as they have continued to evolve and continue to add more and more models to the market.
